title: Error Handling
text: Error Handling Uniface provides an error handling mechanism that allows you to trap errors defined by Uniface, the connector, and the DBMS. Uniface provides an error handling mechanism that allows you to trap the following types of errors: Error classes predefined by Uniface—defined independently for every request. They are documented in the descriptions of the connector requests. Errors defined by the connector Errors defined by the DBMS The connector must detect error situations that meet the descriptions of the predefined error classes. The connector reports the error class to Uniface by means of the connector request return value. By convention, return value zero indicates success and negative return values indicate an error class. Positive return values are allowed with some connector requests; these have a special meaning which is documented for each connector request, as relevant. When the connector returns a predefined error code to Uniface, it must also specify a connector error code or a DBMS error code in the field udrverr in the control block. A number of connector requests (Info mode 0, Message, and Name table) are not allowed to return predefined error codes to Uniface. Also these requests should not change uctrl->udrverr . Generally, DBMS error codes should be passed unchanged to Uniface, since the ProcScript programmer may want to check for DBMS errors using the $dberror ProcScript function. The connector may define its own errors, and reserve error codes for this. The connector is responsible for distinguishing between connector error codes and DBMS error codes. Uniface requests the connector to provide error text for the DBMS error code or connector error code that was passed via udrverr in the control block. The connector may query a DBMS system table containing error messages to translate a DBMS error code to text. Related Links Database Connector Requests A database connector implements a set of generic Uniface requests for the underlying DBMS. Connector requests are well-defined tasks performed by the DBMS connector when it is called. $dberror Return the error code reported by the DBMS. uctrl Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table.
